questions.question: What does the Latin inscription above the columns of the landmark in the image mean?
questions.expected: It states that Marcus Agrippa, son of Lucius, built it when he was consul for the third time.
questions.rgb_answer: The Latin inscription above the columns of the Pantheon reads "MAGRIPPA L.F. COS. III. FECIT," which translates to "Magrippa, son of Lucius Furius, Consul for the third time, built this." This inscription indicates that the structure was built by a person named Magrippa, who was the son of Lucius Furius and held the title of Consul for the third time. The term "L.F." likely stands for "Lucius Furius," which is the name of the family to which the Consul belonged.
